stories.headline: New York just banned new hyperscale data centers for a year
stories.blurb: Governor Kathy Hochul signed the country's first statewide data center moratorium, blocking environmental permits for new hyperscale facilities. A separate bill that could restrict even more developments is still waiting for her signature.
stories.why_it_matters: AI infrastructure buildout just hit its first state-level roadblock in the US.
stories.original_headline: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium

stories.headline: IBM shares plunge after customers shift spend to chips and servers
stories.blurb: IBM reported second-quarter sales below expectations, with CEO acknowledging 'we faltered' as customers redirected budgets toward chips and server infrastructure instead of IBM's traditional software and services offerings.
stories.why_it_matters: Hardware is eating software budgets as companies race to build AI infrastructure.
stories.original_headline: IBM Shares Plunge After Sales Miss, CEO Says ‘We Faltered’

stories.headline: Nous Research raising $75M at $1.5B for its Hermes coding agent
stories.blurb: The startup behind Hermes — an AI coding agent that competes with Cursor and GitHub Copilot — is raising at least $75 million led by Robot Ventures, with Union Square Ventures joining. Nous started as an open-source model shop and pivoted to building developer tools.
stories.why_it_matters: Another sign that investors are betting big on AI coding tools becoming billion-dollar businesses.
stories.original_headline: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation

stories.headline: PixVerse raises $439M to build AI that generates video worlds you can explore
stories.blurb: The video-generation startup just closed a massive round at a $2B+ valuation to expand what it calls its 'world model' — AI that doesn't just generate clips but creates explorable, interactive video environments. The funding will help PixVerse ship the tech to more markets.
stories.why_it_matters: Video AI is shifting from making clips to making explorable spaces you can move through.
stories.original_headline: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B

stories.headline: Apple's new Siri can now control everything on your iPhone
stories.blurb: Apple rebuilt Siri from the ground up in iOS 27. Instead of just answering questions, it now hooks into every app and setting on your phone — you can ask it to adjust settings, navigate apps, or pull data from anywhere. The public beta is live now.
stories.why_it_matters: Siri went from a voice assistant to the main way you use your iPhone.
stories.original_headline: Siri AI Is Becoming Apple’s Everything Tool

stories.headline: DeepMind's CEO calls for a global AI safety body that can halt dangerous models
stories.blurb: Demis Hassabis wants an international watchdog with authority to stop frontier AI development if models become too risky. In a blog post, the Google DeepMind chief argues the US should lead the effort and set global standards, given its position in AI development.
stories.why_it_matters: A DeepMind leader is publicly backing regulatory power over the models his own company builds.
stories.original_headline: Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US

try_this_prompt.title: The negotiation floor-finder
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm negotiating something and I'll describe it below. Don't give me tactics or scripts. Instead: tell me the number or term where I should walk away without hesitation, the psychological reason I'll be tempted to go lower, and the one thing I should ask for if they meet my number.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you counter-offer and accidentally negotiate against yourself.
